project.name: Biostatistics for Physician Researchers
project.description: Interactive biostatistics textbook as a Quarto Book with Observable JS + WebR
project.standards: Hartzog color system, Wong colorblind palette

layer_1_julia_build_time.purpose: Pre-compute datasets, static tables, reference values during quarto render

layer_2_observable_js.purpose: Interactive visualizations (D3 v7, Observable Plot), parameter sliders, animated simulations

layer_3_webr.purpose: User data upload + statistical analysis, exercise grading, live code editing

agentic_workflow.conflict_avoidance: Each chapter is a single .qmd file. No shared files modified during Phase 2. _quarto.yml chapter listing assembled in Phase 3.

foundation.title: Quarto Book Scaffold
tasks: Initialize Quarto Book project: quarto create project book BiostatTextbook

Configure _quarto.yml: 30 chapters in 4 Parts (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced/Expert) + Appendices, HTML format with custom SCSS, dark mode, MathJax, search, Julia engine with --project exeflags, execute cache/freeze
Install quarto-live extension: quarto add r-wasm/live
Create Julia Project.toml with build-time deps: HypothesisTests, GLM, Distributions, StatsBase, DataFrames, CSV, CairoMakie, MultipleTesting, Bootstrap, KernelDensity
Create assets/css/custom.scss: Hartzog colors, Wong palette, custom callout styles (Definition, Formula, Clinical Example, Common Mistake, Key Concept), exercise blocks, print styles
Create chapters/_common-setup.qmd: shared Julia imports + OJS module imports
Create placeholder index.qmd: book overview, tier descriptions, How to Use This Book
Create references.bib with 50+ foundational biostatistics references
Create directory skeleton for all folders
Create placeholder .qmd stubs for all 30 chapters
Verify: quarto render produces complete book with navigation, search, all placeholder chapters
quality_gates: Book renders with TOC, chapter navigation, search functional
Custom theme applies (colors, fonts, dark mode toggle)
All 30 chapter stubs accessible via navigation

foundation.title: Shared OJS Modules & WebR Configuration

features: Descriptive: mean, median, mode, SD, variance, IQR, skewness, kurtosis, percentiles
Tests: one-sample/two-sample/paired t, chi-squared, Fisher exact, correlation, Mann-Whitney
Effect sizes: Cohen's d, Hedges' g, eta-squared, OR, RR, NNT
Confidence intervals: mean (Z/t), proportion (Wilson/Clopper-Pearson), difference, bootstrap
Power approximations for t-test, chi-squared, correlation
Returns standardized TestResult objects: {testName, statistic, pValue, ci, effectSize, effectSizeName, df, n, interpretation}

plot-factory.js.description: Observable Plot + D3 v7 visualization factory
features: scatter(x, y) with regression line + CI band
forestPlot(studies), kmPlot(times, events, groups), rocCurve(truth, scores)
All: Wong colorblind palette, responsive, tooltips, SVG/PNG download
Continuous: mean±SD or median(IQR) based on normality

tasks: Create shared R setup with helper functions
Verify WebR loads and executes R code in browser

quality_gates: All OJS modules import correctly in a test .qmd
stat-engine.js t-test matches R t.test() within 1e-6
WebR loads and runs t.test(rnorm(100)) in browser

foundation.title: Sample Datasets

datasets.description: RCT Drug A vs Placebo for HTN
HbA1c diabetes monitoring, 6 visits, medication groups
Large clinical dataset with LOS, ICU, labs

foundation.generation: Julia script (scripts/generate_datasets.jl) with fixed seed, realistic correlations, <5% missing. DATA_DICTIONARY.md with full variable descriptions.
foundation.validation: R reference scripts (scripts/validate_webr.R) compute reference values for all tests → test/reference_values.json

foundation.title: Landing Page & Appendices

tasks.index.qmd: Full landing page: title, Who This Book Is For, 4 tier cards, How to Use This Book (3 computation layers), Interactive Features teaser demo, Prerequisites, navigation to Ch1
tasks.glossary.qmd: 500+ statistical terms as Quarto definition lists, searchable, cross-referenced to introducing chapters
tasks.formula-reference.qmd: All key formulas by chapter, LaTeX rendered, linked back to source sections
tasks.test-decision-tree.qmd: OJS-powered interactive test selector: outcome type → groups → paired? → assumptions → recommended test + chapter link
tasks.r-code-reference.qmd: WebR code cookbook — common R patterns, copy-paste ready
tasks.datasets.qmd: Dataset documentation with download links and preview tables

quality_gates: Test decision tree produces correct recommendations
R cookbook examples execute in WebR

chapters_beginner.title: Ch1: Types of Data and Measurement Scales
content: 1.1 Why measurement scales matter (determines test choice)
1.2 Nominal (blood type, diagnosis; mode, frequencies, chi-squared)
1.3 Ordinal (cancer stage, NYHA, GCS, APGAR; median, IQR)
1.4 Interval (temperature, IQ; no true zero; mean, SD)
1.5 Ratio (weight, BP, labs; true zero; geometric mean for log-normal)
1.7 Decision framework flowchart + variable-to-test mapping table

ojs_demos.title: Classification Game
ojs_demos.description: 20 clinical variables appear, user selects type, scored

ojs_demos.title: Distribution Shape Explorer
ojs_demos.description: Select column from sample data, auto-detect type, histogram or bar with summary stats

ojs_demos.title: Wrong Test Detector
ojs_demos.description: Run inappropriate test, show why result is meaningless

ojs_demos.title: Variable Audit
ojs_demos.description: Auto-classify all variables in uploaded dataset, override, export codebook

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Upload CSV → auto-classify columns → verify/override types → codebook → variable summary dashboard

chapters_beginner.title: Ch2: Descriptive Statistics
content: Dispersion (range, IQR, variance, SD, CV, MAD)

ojs_demos.title: Mean-Median-Mode Machine
ojs_demos.description: Adjustable distribution + outlier slider, watch mean shift while median stays stable

ojs_demos.title: SD Intuition Builder
ojs_demos.description: Same mean, different SD via slider

ojs_demos.title: Box Plot Anatomy Lab
ojs_demos.description: Hover components, toggle violin/swarm/raincloud

ojs_demos.title: Table 1 Generator
ojs_demos.description: Select variables + grouping, publication-quality table, toggle mean vs median, p-values on/off

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Upload → comprehensive descriptive report + distribution explorer + normality testing + Table 1

chapters_beginner.title: Ch3: Probability Foundations
content: Distributions intro (Binomial, Poisson, Normal, Log-Normal, Exponential)
Normal deep dive (Z-scores, CLT, QQ plots)

ojs_demos.title: Bayes' Theorem Visualizer
ojs_demos.description: Sliders: prevalence, sensitivity, specificity → Fagan nomogram + 1000-patient icon array (TP/FP/TN/FN)

ojs_demos.title: Distribution Explorer
ojs_demos.description: Dropdown distribution type, parameter sliders, PDF/CDF side by side

ojs_demos.title: CLT Simulator
ojs_demos.description: Draw samples from any distribution, animated convergence to Normal — THE signature demo

ojs_demos.title: Screening Test Evaluator
ojs_demos.description: Input sens/spec/prev → icon array + 2×2 table + all diagnostic metrics

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Upload diagnostic data → 2×2 table → Sens/Spec/PPV/NPV/ROC/AUC/optimal cutpoint → distribution fitting

chapters_beginner.title: Ch4: Sampling and Study Design

ojs_demos.title: Study Design Decision Tree
ojs_demos.description: Answer questions about research goal → recommended design + checklist

ojs_demos.title: Sampling Simulator
ojs_demos.description: 10K-dot population, compare sampling methods, show bias + precision

ojs_demos.title: Bias Illustrated
ojs_demos.description: 6 interactive scenarios with DAGs and mitigation

ojs_demos.title: Randomization Validator
ojs_demos.description: Simulate randomization, show covariate balance (Love plot)

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Study Design Advisor; Covariate Balance Checker

chapters_beginner.title: Ch5: Confidence Intervals

ojs_demos.title: CI Interpretation Simulator
ojs_demos.description: Draw 100 CIs, ~5 miss true parameter, toggle 90/95/99%

ojs_demos.title: CI Width Explorer
ojs_demos.description: 3 sliders: n, confidence level, SD

ojs_demos.title: Bootstrap CI Machine
ojs_demos.description: Animated resampling, build distribution, show percentile + BCa

ojs_demos.title: Forest Plot Builder
ojs_demos.description: Add/remove studies, see pooled estimate change

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: CI Calculator (parametric + bootstrap); Precision-Based Sample Size

chapters_beginner.title: Ch6: Hypothesis Testing Framework
content: Statistical vs clinical significance (MCID, effect sizes, superiority/non-inferiority/equivalence)

ojs_demos.title: Hypothesis Test Visualizer
ojs_demos.description: Two overlapping Normals (H₀/H₁), shade α/β/power regions, sliders: μ₀, μ₁, σ, n, α — THE key demo

ojs_demos.title: P-Value Simulator
ojs_demos.description: 10,000 experiments under H₀ (uniform) vs H₁ (right-skewed)

ojs_demos.title: Multiple Testing Disaster
ojs_demos.description: 20 tests on random data, show false positives, apply corrections

ojs_demos.title: Clinical Significance Detector
ojs_demos.description: CI relative to zero AND MCID, four outcome zones

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Hypothesis Test Advisor; P-Value Context Report (effect size + CI + power)

chapters_beginner.title: Ch7: Visualizing Medical Data
content: Tufte principles (data-ink ratio, chartjunk, lie factor)
Common mistakes (truncated axis, dual axis, pie charts, 3D, rainbow)
Publication standards (300 DPI, vector vs raster)

ojs_demos.title: Plot Type Chooser
ojs_demos.description: Interactive decision tree → recommended plot

ojs_demos.title: Visualization Playground
ojs_demos.description: Select variables, map aesthetics, choose type, customize theme/palette — live Observable Plot

ojs_demos.description: Input true ratio + visual ratio → compute lie factor

ojs_demos.title: Clinical Plot Gallery
ojs_demos.description: 20+ clinical plot types with live examples

chapters_beginner.webr_analysis: Auto-Visualization (recommended plots for all variables); Custom Figure Builder with ggplot2
